stemHtml: A pilot is planning to depart from an airfield at sea level in the southern hemisphere below a layer of nimbo-stratus cloud with ceiling 5000 ft, the surface temperature at the airfield is +3°C, +7°C at the cloud ceiling, and it is raining. The aircraft`s initial track will be towards a nearby mountain range. Which of the following phenomena might indicate a risk of icing outside of the cloud shortly after take-off?

options.a: A SIGMET reporting severe turbulence in the lower levels of the atmosphere associated with the nearby mountains.
options.b: The forecast northerly wind backing with altitude and becoming easterly.
options.c: The dew point being reported as below the temperature at the airfield.
options.d: A temperature inversion aloft between 2000 ft and 5000 ft where the temperature at 2000 ft is -4°C.
